teaching_arc.entry_point: Session 3 is complete, derivatives now answer how fast linked quantities change in geometry and physics.
teaching_arc.progression: Four-step workflow → warm-ups (square, balloon) → Pythagorean ladder → similar-triangle shadow & cone → applications → common mistake → lab → close.
teaching_arc.destination: Students set up related-rates problems: symbolic link, differentiate w.r.t. t, substitute last.

topic_goal: Teach related rates as implicit differentiation with respect to time, using chain rule callbacks from Topics 13–14.
topic_takeaways: Four-step workflow: draw, link, differentiate w.r.t. t, substitute last
Chain rule links rates of variables sharing a clock
Never substitute numeric values before differentiating
Templates: square area, balloon volume, Pythagorean ladder, shadow, cone, radar
style_notes: Welcome back, Session 3 complete; Session 4 opens applications of derivatives.
Callback Topic 13 chain rule and Topic 14 implicit differentiation on t.
Roadmap currentId path_topic_15; Topic 16 Critical Points next.
Narrate flex_plot probes before revealing algebra on math_solution_steps slides.
YouTube pauses on s03, s05, s08, s10, real think beats, not decorative.
visual_lab s13: demo one tab ~30s on YouTube; mention site tabs.
transition_style: Move from changing-world hook to workflow, then ladder/shadow/cone worked examples, then mistake + lab + close.

slides.narration_seed: Welcome back, Session 3 derivative rules are done. Topic 15 opens Session 4: related rates, how fast linked quantities change.
Roadmap: Topics 8–14 done. Five objectives, workflow, chain rule, ladder, shadow, cone.
Balloon metaphor. step_1 differentiate V=4/3 pi r^3. step_2 solve dr/dt. step_3 evaluate at r=5. Graph: r linear, V cubic.
must_say: Everything changes with respect to the same clock t.

slides.narration_seed: theorem_box chain rule for time. Four nested_bullets workflow. Circle area rate example on right.
step_1 dA/dt=2s ds/dt. step_2 substitute s=5, ds/dt=3. step_3 answer 30. Pause: substituting before differentiating trap.
x^2+y^2=z^2 differentiate w.r.t. t. Do not substitute before differentiating. Ladder triangle graph.
Pause, 10-ft ladder, dx/dt=2, x=6. Hint reveal: 2x xdot+2y ydot=0, y=8, expect negative ydot.
step_1 differentiate. step_2 solve dy/dt. step_3 y=8. step_4 dy/dt=-1.5 ft/s. Sign means downward.
Similar triangles for cone and shadow. Shadow: s=5/3 x, ds/dt=25/3 at x=10. Cone ratio r/h constant.
step_1 V cone + r=h/3. step_2 V=pi/27 h^3. step_3 differentiate. step_4 dh/dt about 0.398. Pause estimate.
Optional: as x→10, dy/dt→-∞. Model assumes top stays on wall, fails physically.
s^2=x^2+2500. step_4 dx/dt about 86.67 ft/s ≈ 59 mph. Radar measures ds/dt not dx/dt.
misconception_compare: plug x=6,y=8 first gives 0=0 vs differentiate first then substitute.
must_say: Differentiate the general equation before substituting.

slides.narration_seed: Optional IFT: F_x dx/dt+F_y dy/dt=0. Same as Topic 14. Circle constraint graph.
visual_lab, square, circle area, cone tabs. YouTube: demo square tab briefly.
Six bullets + final check dA/dt=24. Teaser Topic 16 Critical Points. Thank students.
must_say: Next topic: Critical Points and Extrema on an Interval.
